tools.name: cli_execute_command
tools.description: Execute CLI commands from JavaScript post-actions. Base whitelist: git, gh, dmtools, npm, yarn, docker, kubectl, terraform, ansible, aws, gcloud, az. Additional commands can be enabled via CLI_ALLOWED_COMMANDS env var (comma-separated) in dmtools.env or agent envVariables, e.g. CLI_ALLOWED_COMMANDS=find,ls,cat,pytest,python3,curl,bash,run-cursor-agent.sh. Returns command output as string.

workingDirectory.description: Working directory for command execution. Defaults to repository root if not specified. Use absolute path or path relative to current directory.

command.description: CLI command to execute. Must start with a whitelisted command. Extend the whitelist via CLI_ALLOWED_COMMANDS in dmtools.env.

tools.name: github_test
tools.description: Test GitHub connectivity by fetching the current user's profile

tools.name: github_list_prs
tools.description: List pull requests in a GitHub repository by state. State can be 'open', 'closed', or 'merged'. Returns first page (up to 100) of pull requests.

state.description: The state of pull requests to list: 'open', 'closed', or 'merged'. 'opened' is accepted as a synonym for 'open'.

tools.name: github_list_prs_filtered
tools.description: List pull requests in a GitHub repository filtered by a regex pattern on the PR title. Fetches all PRs matching the given state and returns only those whose title matches the regex. Useful for large repos to narrow down results without loading entire history.

titleRegex.description: Java regular expression matched against the PR title (case-sensitive). Only PRs whose title contains a match are returned. Example: '^feat\(.*\)' or 'TICKET-\d+'.

state.description: The state of pull requests: 'open', 'closed', or 'merged'.

tools.name: github_get_commits_from_branches
tools.description: Fetch commits from all branches whose name matches a given regex pattern, aggregated and de-duplicated. Useful for collecting commits from feature/*, release/* or similar groups of branches without specifying each branch individually.

branchNameRegex.description: Java regular expression matched against branch names. All branches with a matching name are included. Example: '^feature/' or 'release/\d+'.

since.description: Optional ISO date (yyyy-MM-dd) to limit commits to those after this date.

tools.name: github_get_pr
tools.description: Get details of a GitHub pull request including title, description, status, author, branches, and merge info.

tools.name: github_repository_dispatch
tools.description: Trigger a GitHub repository dispatch event. Workflows listening to 'on: repository_dispatch' with the matching event_type will be triggered.

eventType.description: The type of activity that triggers the workflow (event_type)

clientPayload.description: Optional JSON string with payload passed to the workflow as client_payload

tools.name: github_trigger_workflow
tools.description: Trigger a specific GitHub Actions workflow by filename (workflow dispatch). The workflow must have 'on: workflow_dispatch' configured.

ref.description: The branch or tag to run the workflow on (default: main)

workflowId.description: The workflow filename or ID to trigger

tools.name: github_get_or_create_draft_release
tools.description: Find an existing draft release by tag or name, or create one if it does not exist. Useful for a stable PR attachment storage release.

tagName.description: The Git tag name for the release. Reused to find an existing draft release.

targetCommitish.description: Optional branch or commit SHA the release should point to when created.

body.example: Internal storage release for PR attachments.

releaseName.description: The human-readable release name. If empty, tagName is used.

tools.name: github_upload_release_asset
tools.description: Upload a local file as a GitHub release asset. Returns the uploaded asset metadata including browser_download_url. Set overwrite=true to automatically delete an existing asset with the same name before uploading.

releaseId.description: The numeric GitHub release ID returned by github_get_or_create_draft_release.

filePath.description: Absolute or relative path to the local file to upload.

assetName.description: Optional asset filename shown in GitHub. Defaults to the local filename.

label.description: Optional display label for the uploaded asset.

contentType.description: Optional MIME type. Defaults to detected type or application/octet-stream.

overwrite.description: If true, delete any existing asset with the same name before uploading. Defaults to false.

tools.name: github_delete_release_asset
tools.description: Delete a GitHub release asset by its asset ID. Use github_list_release_assets to find asset IDs.

assetId.description: The numeric asset ID to delete.

tools.name: github_list_release_assets
tools.description: List all assets attached to a GitHub release. Returns a JSON array of asset objects including id, name, size, and browser_download_url.

tools.name: github_get_pr_comments
tools.description: Get all comments for a GitHub pull request, including both inline code review comments and general discussion comments. Results are sorted by creation date.

tools.name: github_get_pr_conversations
tools.description: Get all review conversations (inline code comment threads) for a GitHub pull request. Groups inline code review comments into threads showing root comment and replies. Also includes general PR discussion comments as separate entries.

tools.name: github_get_pr_activities
tools.description: Get all activities for a GitHub pull request including reviews (approvals, change requests), inline code comments, and general discussion comments.

tools.name: github_add_pr_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to a GitHub pull request discussion.

tools.name: github_update_pr_comment
tools.description: Update (edit) an existing comment on a GitHub pull request or issue by its comment ID.

commentId.description: The ID of the comment to update

text.description: The new comment text (replaces existing content)

tools.name: github_delete_pr_comment
tools.description: Delete a comment on a GitHub pull request or issue by its comment ID.

commentId.description: The ID of the comment to delete

tools.name: github_reply_to_pr_thread
tools.description: Reply to an existing inline code review comment thread in a GitHub pull request. Use the comment ID of the root comment (or any comment) in the thread as inReplyToId.

inReplyToId.description: The ID of the comment to reply to (from github_get_pr_conversations rootComment.id or replies[].id)

tools.name: github_add_inline_comment
tools.description: Create a new inline code review comment on a specific file and line in a GitHub pull request. To comment on a range of lines, provide both startLine and line. Side is 'RIGHT' for new code (default) or 'LEFT' for old code.

path.description: The relative file path in the repository

side.description: Which diff side to comment on: RIGHT (new code, default) or LEFT (old code)

line.description: The line number in the file to comment on

startLine.description: For multi-line comments: the first line of the range. Must be less than line.

commitId.description: The SHA of the commit to comment on. If empty, uses the PR head commit.

tools.name: github_get_pr_review_threads
tools.description: Get all review threads for a GitHub pull request via GraphQL, including each thread's node ID (needed for resolving), resolved status, file path, line, and comments. Use the returned thread 'id' with github_resolve_pr_thread.

tools.name: github_resolve_pr_thread
tools.description: Resolve a review thread in a GitHub pull request. Requires the thread's GraphQL node ID, which can be obtained from github_get_pr_review_threads (the 'id' field of each thread).

threadId.description: The GraphQL node ID of the review thread to resolve (from github_get_pr_review_threads thread.id)

tools.name: github_get_commit_check_runs
tools.description: Get all check runs (CI/CD status checks) for a commit SHA in a GitHub repository. Returns details about each check including status, conclusion, and output.

commitSha.description: The commit SHA to get check runs for

tools.name: github_create_commit_status
tools.description: Create a commit status (the colored dot in PR checks). Use state=pending when AI analysis starts, success/failure/error when complete. The 'context' field acts as the status name and must be unique per check.

context.description: Unique identifier for this status check, e.g. 'dmtools/pr-review'

description.description: Short human-readable description shown next to the status dot

state.description: The state: pending | success | failure | error

sha.description: The commit SHA to set status on

targetUrl.description: Optional URL to link from the status (e.g. CI run URL)

tools.name: github_create_check_run
tools.description: Create a GitHub Check Run — a rich CI check with progress, annotations, and a full log visible in the PR 'Checks' tab. Use status=in_progress when starting, then call github_update_check_run to complete it.

summary.description: Markdown summary shown in the check run output panel

name.description: The name of the check run displayed in the PR

externalId.description: Optional external identifier for this check run

headSha.description: The SHA of the commit to associate this check run with

text.description: Additional details in Markdown (supports large content)

title.description: Title shown in the check run output panel

tools.name: github_update_check_run
tools.description: Update an existing GitHub Check Run — set it to completed with success/failure conclusion, update summary and detailed text. Call this after github_create_check_run to finalize the check.

conclusion.description: Required when status=completed: success | failure | neutral | cancelled | skipped | timed_out | action_required

summary.description: Updated Markdown summary for the check run output panel

checkRunId.description: The ID of the check run to update (from github_create_check_run response)

text.description: Updated detailed Markdown content (full analysis, annotations etc.)

title.description: Updated title for the check run output panel

tools.name: github_get_workflow_run
tools.description: Get details of a specific GitHub Actions workflow run by ID. Returns status, conclusion, logs URL, and timing information.

tools.name: github_get_workflow_run_jobs
tools.description: Get all jobs for a specific GitHub Actions workflow run. Shows individual job statuses, steps, and logs URLs.

tools.name: github_list_workflow_runs
tools.description: List GitHub Actions workflow runs for a repository, optionally filtered by status or specific workflow file. Use status='failure' to get all failed runs.

perPage.description: Number of results per page (max 100, default 30)

created.description: Filter by created date/range using GitHub search syntax, e.g. 2026-05-01..2026-05-31 or >=2026-05-01

workflowId.description: Optional workflow filename to filter runs (e.g. rework.yml). If omitted, returns runs for all workflows.

status.description: Filter by status: failure, success, in_progress, queued, cancelled, timed_out, action_required, neutral, skipped, stale, completed

tools.name: github_get_job_logs
tools.description: Get the raw text logs for a specific GitHub Actions job. Returns the complete log output from all steps in the job.

tools.name: github_get_workflow_run_logs
tools.description: Download and extract complete logs for all jobs in a GitHub Actions workflow run. Returns full untruncated log content from the ZIP archive GitHub provides.

tools.name: github_add_pr_label
tools.description: Add a label to a GitHub pull request.

label.description: The label name to add to the pull request

tools.name: github_remove_pr_label
tools.description: Remove a label from a GitHub pull request.

label.description: The label name to remove from the pull request

tools.name: github_merge_pr
tools.description: Merge a GitHub pull request. Supports merge, squash, and rebase merge methods.

pullRequestId.description: The pull request number to merge

commitMessage.description: Extra detail to append to the merge commit message (optional)

commitTitle.description: Title for the merge commit (optional, defaults to PR title)

tools.name: github_get_pr_diff
tools.description: Get the diff statistics for a GitHub pull request (files changed, additions, deletions). Requires IS_READ_PULL_REQUEST_DIFF env/config to be enabled.

tools.name: github_get_pr_diff_text
tools.description: Get the raw unified diff text for a GitHub pull request. Requires IS_READ_PULL_REQUEST_DIFF env/config to be enabled.

tools.name: confluence_contents_by_urls
tools.description: Get Confluence content by multiple URLs. Returns a list of content objects for each valid URL. Use format=md to convert body.storage.value to Markdown.

format.description: Output format for the page body. Use 'md' or 'markdown' to convert Confluence storage format to Markdown.

urlStrings.description: Array of Confluence URLs to retrieve content from

tools.name: confluence_search_content_by_text
tools.description: Search Confluence content by text query using CQL (Confluence Query Language). Returns search results with content excerpts. Default limit is 20 if not specified.

limit.description: Maximum number of search results to return. Default is 20 if not provided.

query.description: Search query text to find in Confluence content

tools.name: confluence_content_by_id
tools.description: Get Confluence content by its unique content ID. Returns detailed content information including body, version, and metadata. Use format=md to convert body.storage.value to Markdown.

contentId.description: The unique content ID of the Confluence page

format.description: Output format for the page body. Use 'md' or 'markdown' to convert Confluence storage format to Markdown.

tools.name: confluence_content_by_title_and_space
tools.description: Get Confluence content by title and space key. Returns content result with metadata and body information. Use format=md to convert body.storage.value to Markdown.

title.description: The title of the Confluence page

format.description: Output format for the page body. Use 'md' or 'markdown' to convert Confluence storage format to Markdown.

space.description: The space key where the content is located

tools.name: confluence_test
tools.description: Test Confluence connectivity by fetching the current user's profile

tools.name: confluence_get_current_user_profile
tools.description: Get the current user's profile information from Confluence. Returns user details for the authenticated user.

tools.name: confluence_get_user_profile_by_id
tools.description: Get a specific user's profile information from Confluence by user ID. Returns user details for the specified user.

userId.description: The account ID of the user to get profile for

tools.name: confluence_get_content_attachments
tools.description: Get all attachments for a specific Confluence content. Returns a list of attachment objects with metadata.

contentId.description: The content ID to get attachments for

tools.name: confluence_find_content_by_title_and_space
tools.description: Find Confluence content by title and space key. Returns the first matching content or null if not found. Use format=md to convert body.storage.value to Markdown.

title.description: The title of the content to find

format.description: Output format for the page body. Use 'md' or 'markdown' to convert Confluence storage format to Markdown.

space.description: The space key where to search for the content

tools.name: confluence_create_page
tools.description: Create a new Confluence page with specified title, parent, body content, and space. Returns the created content object.

title.description: The title of the new page

body.description: The body content of the page in Confluence storage format

parentId.description: The ID of the parent page

space.description: The space key where to create the page

tools.name: confluence_update_page
tools.description: Update an existing Confluence page with new title, parent, body content, and space. Returns the updated content object.

contentId.description: The ID of the page to update

title.description: The new title for the page

body.description: The new body content of the page in Confluence storage format

parentId.description: The ID of the new parent page

space.description: The space key where the page is located

tools.name: confluence_update_page_with_history
tools.description: Update an existing Confluence page with new content and add a history comment. Returns the updated content object.

contentId.description: The ID of the page to update

title.description: The new title for the page

body.description: The new body content of the page in Confluence storage format

parentId.description: The ID of the new parent page

space.description: The space key where the page is located

historyComment.description: Comment to add to the page history
historyComment.example: Updated content based on user feedback

tools.name: confluence_get_children_by_name
tools.description: Get child pages of a Confluence page by space key and content name. Returns a list of child content objects. Use format=md to convert body.storage.value to Markdown.

spaceKey.description: The space key where the parent page is located

format.description: Output format for the page body. Use 'md' or 'markdown' to convert Confluence storage format to Markdown.

contentName.description: The name/title of the parent page

tools.name: confluence_get_children_by_id
tools.description: Get child pages of a Confluence page by content ID. Returns a list of child content objects. Use format=md to convert body.storage.value to Markdown.

contentId.description: The content ID of the parent page

format.description: Output format for the page body. Use 'md' or 'markdown' to convert Confluence storage format to Markdown.

tools.name: confluence_download_attachment
tools.description: Download an attachment file from Confluence to a specified directory.

targetDir.description: The target directory to save the file

tools.name: confluence_download_pages
tools.description: Download Confluence pages and their attachments to a local folder. Recursively follows linked pages, children macros, and internal ac:link references up to the specified depth.

urlStrings.description: Array of Confluence page URLs to download

depth.description: How many levels of linked/child pages to follow. Default is 1.

downloadAttachments.description: Whether to download page attachments. Default is true.

outputPath.description: Local folder path where pages and attachments will be saved

tools.name: confluence_find_content
tools.description: Find a Confluence page by title in the default space. Returns the page content if found. Use format=md to convert body.storage.value to Markdown.

title.description: Title of the Confluence page to find

format.description: Output format for the page body. Use 'md' or 'markdown' to convert Confluence storage format to Markdown.

tools.name: confluence_find_or_create
tools.description: Find a Confluence page by title in the default space, or create it if it doesn't exist. Returns the found or created content.

title.description: Title of the page to find or create

body.description: Body content for the new page (if creation is needed)

parentId.description: ID of the parent page for creation

tools.name: confluence_content_by_title
tools.description: Get Confluence content by title in the default space. Returns content result with metadata and body information. Use format=md to convert body.storage.value to Markdown.

title.description: Title of the Confluence page to get

format.description: Output format for the page body. Use 'md' or 'markdown' to convert Confluence storage format to Markdown.

tools.description: Test Azure DevOps connectivity by fetching the current user's profile

tools.name: ado_get_my_profile
tools.description: Get the current user's profile information from Azure DevOps

tools.name: ado_list_prs
tools.description: List pull requests in an Azure DevOps Git repository by status. Status can be 'active', 'completed', or 'abandoned'.

status.description: The status of pull requests to list: 'active', 'completed', or 'abandoned'. 'open'/'opened' accepted as synonym for 'active'.

tools.name: ado_get_pr
tools.description: Get details of an Azure DevOps pull request including title, description, status, author, reviewers, branches, and merge info.

tools.name: ado_get_pr_comments
tools.description: Get all comment threads for an Azure DevOps pull request. Each thread contains comments, file context for inline comments, and status (active, fixed, closed, etc.).

tools.name: ado_add_pr_comment
tools.description: Add a general comment to an Azure DevOps pull request (creates a new thread). For inline code comments, use ado_add_inline_comment instead.

text.description: The comment text to add (Markdown supported)

tools.name: ado_reply_to_pr_thread
tools.description: Reply to an existing comment thread in an Azure DevOps pull request. Use the threadId from ado_get_pr_comments.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread to reply to (from ado_get_pr_comments)

tools.name: ado_add_inline_comment
tools.description: Create a new inline code comment on a specific file and line range in an Azure DevOps pull request. Creates a new thread with file context.

side.description: Which diff side to comment on: 'right' (new code, default) or 'left' (old code)

line.description: The line number to comment on (1-based)

filePath.description: The relative file path in the repository (must start with /)

startLine.description: For multi-line comments: the first line of the range. Must be less than or equal to line.

tools.name: ado_resolve_pr_thread
tools.description: Resolve (close) a comment thread in an Azure DevOps pull request. Sets the thread status to 'fixed'. Other statuses: 'active', 'closed', 'byDesign', 'pending', 'wontFix'.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread to resolve (from ado_get_pr_comments)

tools.name: ado_update_pr_comment
tools.description: Update (edit) an existing comment in a pull request thread. Requires both threadId and commentId.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread containing the comment

commentId.description: The ID of the comment to update

text.description: The new comment text (replaces existing content)

tools.name: ado_delete_pr_comment
tools.description: Delete a comment from a pull request thread. Requires both threadId and commentId.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread containing the comment

commentId.description: The ID of the comment to delete

tools.name: ado_get_pr_diff
tools.description: Get the diff/changes for an Azure DevOps pull request. Returns the list of changed files with change types.

tools.name: ado_merge_pr
tools.description: Complete (merge) an Azure DevOps pull request. Sets status to 'completed' with the specified merge strategy.

pullRequestId.description: The pull request ID to complete/merge

deleteSourceBranch.description: Whether to delete the source branch after merging (default: true)

tools.name: ado_add_pr_label
tools.description: Add a label (tag) to an Azure DevOps pull request.

tools.name: ado_remove_pr_label
tools.description: Remove a label (tag) from an Azure DevOps pull request. Requires the labelId (from ado_get_pr or ado_list_prs labels array).

labelId.description: The label ID to remove (from the PR labels array, not the label name)

tools.name: ado_get_pr_reviewers
tools.description: Get all reviewers for an Azure DevOps pull request with their vote status. Vote: 10=approved, 5=approved with suggestions, 0=no vote, -5=waiting for author, -10=rejected.

tools.name: ado_add_pr_reviewer
tools.description: Add a reviewer to an Azure DevOps pull request. Optionally set their initial vote.

reviewerId.description: The reviewer's ID (GUID from ado_get_user_by_email or uniqueName)

vote.description: Optional initial vote: 10=approve, 5=approve with suggestions, 0=no vote, -5=wait for author, -10=reject

tools.name: ado_set_pr_vote
tools.description: Set the current user's vote on a pull request. Vote values: 10=approve, 5=approve with suggestions, 0=reset/no vote, -5=wait for author, -10=reject.

reviewerId.description: The reviewer's ID (GUID) — use ado_get_my_profile or ado_get_user_by_email to get it

vote.description: Vote value: 10=approve, 5=approve with suggestions, 0=reset, -5=wait for author, -10=reject

tools.name: ado_update_pr
tools.description: Update pull request properties such as title, description, or status. Use status='abandoned' to abandon a PR, or 'active' to reactivate.

description.description: New description for the pull request (Markdown supported)

title.description: New title for the pull request

tools.name: ado_get_pr_work_items
tools.description: Get work items linked to an Azure DevOps pull request.

tools.name: ado_list_pipelines
tools.description: List all pipelines defined in the ADO project.

tools.name: ado_trigger_pipeline
tools.description: Trigger a pipeline run in ADO. Equivalent to github_trigger_workflow.

branch.description: The branch to run the pipeline on (e.g. 'main')

variables.description: JSON object of pipeline variables, e.g. {"myVar":"value"}

tools.name: ado_list_pipeline_runs
tools.description: List recent runs of a pipeline. Equivalent to github_list_workflow_runs.

tools.name: ado_get_pipeline_run
tools.description: Get details of a specific pipeline run including state and result.

tools.name: ado_get_pipeline_logs
tools.description: Get combined logs for all tasks in a pipeline run (build ID). Equivalent to github_get_job_logs.

tailLines.description: Lines to return from the end of each task log (default 200, 0 = all)

buildId.description: The build/run ID returned by ado_trigger_pipeline or ado_list_pipeline_runs

taskName.description: Optional: filter logs to a specific task name (case-insensitive substring match)

tools.name: mermaid_index_generate
tools.description: Generate Mermaid diagrams from content sources (Confluence or Jira) based on include/exclude patterns. Processes content recursively and stores diagrams in hierarchical file structure.

include_patterns.description: Array of include patterns. For Confluence: ["SPACE/pages/PAGE_ID/PAGE_NAME/**"]. For Jira: ["JQL query"]. For TestRail: ["project_id=5&suite_id=3"]

exclude_patterns.description: Optional array of exclude patterns to filter out specific content (not used for Jira)

storage_path.description: Base path for storing generated diagrams

custom_fields.description: Optional array of custom field names to include in content (only for Jira integrations)

include_comments.description: Whether to include comments in content (only for Jira integrations, default: false)

tools.name: mermaid_index_read_list
tools.description: Read all Mermaid diagram files (.mmd) from storage path recursively. Returns list of ToText objects with paths and content.

integration.description: Integration type (used as subfolder name under storage path)

storage_path.description: Base path where diagrams are stored

tools.name: mermaid_index_read
tools.description: Read all Mermaid diagram files (.mmd) from storage path recursively. Returns list of diagrams with their paths and content.

integration.description: Integration type (used as subfolder name under storage path)

storage_path.description: Base path where diagrams are stored

tools.name: jira_xray_test
tools.description: Test X-ray connectivity by obtaining an access token

tools.name: jira_xray_create_precondition
tools.description: Create a Precondition issue in Xray with optional steps (converted to definition). Returns the created ticket key.

steps.description: Optional JSON array of steps in format [{"action": "...", "data": "...", "result": "..."}]. Will be converted to definition format.

tools.name: jira_xray_search_tickets
tools.description: Search for Jira tickets using JQL query and enrich Test/Precondition issues with X-ray test steps and preconditions. Returns list of tickets with X-ray data.

fields.description: Array of field names to retrieve (e.g., ['summary', 'description', 'status'])

searchQueryJQL.description: JQL search query (e.g., 'project = TP AND issueType = Test')

tools.name: jira_xray_get_test_details
tools.description: Get test details including steps and preconditions using X-ray GraphQL API. Returns JSONObject with test details.

tools.name: jira_xray_get_test_steps
tools.description: Get test steps for a test issue using X-ray GraphQL API. Returns JSONArray of test steps.

tools.name: jira_xray_get_preconditions
tools.description: Get preconditions for a test issue using X-ray GraphQL API. Returns JSONArray of precondition objects.

tools.name: jira_xray_get_precondition_details
tools.description: Get Precondition details including definition using X-ray GraphQL API. Returns JSONObject with precondition details.

tools.name: jira_xray_add_test_step
tools.description: Add a single test step to a test issue using X-ray GraphQL API. Returns JSONObject with created step details.

tools.name: jira_xray_add_test_steps
tools.description: Add multiple test steps to a test issue using X-ray GraphQL API. Returns JSONArray of created step objects.

steps.description: JSON array string of step objects, each with 'action', 'data', and 'result' fields (e.g., '[{"action":"Enter username","data":"test_user","result":"Username accepted"}]')

tools.name: jira_xray_add_precondition_to_test
tools.description: Add a single precondition to a test issue using X-ray GraphQL API. Returns JSONObject with result.

testIssueId.description: Jira issue ID of the test (e.g., '12345')

preconditionIssueId.description: Jira issue ID of the precondition (e.g., '12346')

tools.name: jira_xray_add_preconditions_to_test
tools.description: Add multiple preconditions to a test issue using X-ray GraphQL API. Returns JSONArray of results.

preconditionIssueIds.description: JSON array string of precondition issue IDs (e.g., '["12346", "12347"]')

testIssueId.description: Jira issue ID of the test (e.g., '12345')

tools.name: file_read
tools.description: Read file content from working directory (supports input/ and outputs/ folders). Returns file content as string or null if file doesn't exist or is inaccessible. All file formats supported as UTF-8 text.

path.description: File path relative to working directory or absolute path within working directory

tools.name: file_write
tools.description: Write content to file in working directory. Creates parent directories automatically. Returns success message or null on failure.

path.description: File path relative to working directory or absolute path within working directory

content.description: Content to write to the file as UTF-8 string

tools.name: file_delete
tools.description: Delete file or directory from working directory. Returns success message or null on failure.

path.description: File path relative to working directory or absolute path within working directory

tools.name: file_validate_json
tools.description: Validate JSON string and return detailed error information if invalid. Returns JSON string with validation result: {"valid": true} for valid JSON, or {"valid": false, "error": "error message", "line": line_number, "column": column_number, "position": character_position, "context": "context around error"} for invalid JSON.

tools.name: file_validate_json_file
tools.description: Validate JSON file and return detailed error information if invalid. Reads file from working directory and validates its JSON content. Returns JSON string with validation result including file path.

path.description: File path relative to working directory or absolute path within working directory

tools.name: figma_oauth2_get_auth_url
tools.description: Generates the Figma OAuth2 authorization URL for the initial authorization code flow. Open the returned URL in a browser, authorize the app, and copy the 'code' parameter from the redirect URL. Then call figma_oauth2_exchange_code to get access and refresh tokens. Requires FIGMA_CLIENT_ID and FIGMA_CLIENT_SECRET to be configured. Optional scope can be passed explicitly or via FIGMA_SCOPE/FIGMA_OAUTH_SCOPES env variable.

redirectUri.description: Redirect URI registered in your Figma OAuth app (e.g. http://localhost:8080/callback). If omitted, uses FIGMA_REDIRECT_URI env variable.

state.description: Random state string for CSRF protection

scope.description: Optional OAuth scope list (space-separated), e.g. file_content:read file_metadata:read. If omitted, uses FIGMA_SCOPE (or FIGMA_OAUTH_SCOPES) env or default minimal read scope.

tools.name: figma_oauth2_exchange_code
tools.description: Exchanges a Figma OAuth2 authorization code for access and refresh tokens. Use the code from the redirect URL after completing the browser authorization flow started by figma_oauth2_get_auth_url. Store FIGMA_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN from the response in your dmtools.env to enable automatic token refresh.

redirectUri.description: Same redirect URI used in figma_oauth2_get_auth_url. If omitted, uses FIGMA_REDIRECT_URI env variable.

code.description: Authorization code received from Figma OAuth2 redirect

tools.name: figma_test
tools.description: Test Figma connectivity by fetching the current user's profile

tools.name: figma_me
tools.description: Gets current user information from the Figma API using the /me endpoint. Returns user details including id, handle, and email. Can also be used to verify API connectivity.

tools.name: figma_get_screen_source
tools.description: Get screen source content by URL. Returns the image URL for the specified Figma design node.

url.description: Figma design URL with node-id parameter

tools.name: figma_download_node_image
tools.description: Download image of specific node/component. Useful for visual preview of design pieces before processing structure.

tools.name: figma_download_image_of_file
tools.description: Download image by URL as File type. Converts Figma design URL to downloadable image file.

href.description: Figma design URL to download as image file

tools.name: figma_get_file_structure
tools.description: Get full JSON structure of a Figma design file by URL. Returns the complete document tree (nodes, frames, components, text, styles). Response is large by design — intended for pre-CLI artifact preparation and file output, not inline AI context. If the URL contains node-id, returns only that subtree.

tools.name: figma_get_icons
tools.description: Find and extract all exportable visual elements (vectors, shapes, graphics, text) from Figma design by URL. Focuses on actual visual elements to avoid complex component references.

href.description: Figma design URL to extract visual elements from

tools.name: figma_get_image_fills
tools.description: Get original image fill URLs for all imageRefs in a Figma file. Resolves imageRef placeholders to actual downloadable S3 URLs. Use after inspecting file structure to download original photos/images embedded by the designer.

tools.name: figma_render_nodes
tools.description: Render multiple Figma nodes as images in a single batched API call. Automatically batches up to 100 node IDs per request. Returns map of nodeId to render URL. Use for exporting many icons or frames efficiently.

format.description: Export format: png, jpg, svg, pdf. Default: png

tools.name: figma_download_image_as_file
tools.description: Download image as file by node ID and format. Use this after figma_get_icons to download actual icon files.

nodeId.description: Node ID to export (from figma_get_icons result)

href.description: Figma design URL to extract file ID from

tools.name: figma_get_svg_content
tools.description: Get SVG content as text by node ID. Use this after figma_get_icons to get SVG code for vector icons.

nodeId.description: Node ID to export as SVG (from figma_get_icons result)

href.description: Figma design URL to extract file ID from

tools.name: figma_get_node_details
tools.description: Get detailed properties for specific node(s) including colors, fonts, text, dimensions, and styles. Returns small focused response.

tools.name: figma_get_text_content
tools.description: Extract text content from text nodes. Returns map of nodeId to text content.

tools.name: figma_get_styles
tools.description: Get design tokens (colors, text styles) defined in Figma file.

tools.name: figma_get_layers
tools.description: Get first-level layers (direct children) to understand structure. Returns layer names, IDs, types, sizes. Essential first step before getting details.

tools.name: figma_get_layers_batch
tools.description: Get layers for multiple nodes at once. More efficient for analyzing multiple screens/containers. Returns map of nodeId to layers.

tools.name: figma_get_node_children
tools.description: Get immediate children IDs and basic info for a node. Non-recursive, returns only direct children.

tools.name: figma_list_team_projects
tools.description: List all projects within a Figma team, so a team-level 'files' listing URL (which is not a single design file and cannot be passed to file-specific tools like figma_get_layers) can be broken down into browsable projects. Accepts either a raw numeric team ID or any Figma URL containing a '/team/<teamId>' path segment.

teamIdOrUrl.description: Numeric Figma team ID, or a Figma URL containing '/team/<teamId>' (e.g. a team files-listing URL)

tools.name: figma_list_project_files
tools.description: List all design files within a Figma project, returning each file's key, name, and thumbnail so a specific file URL can be built for use with file-specific tools like figma_get_layers or figma_get_file_structure. Use figma_list_team_projects first to discover project IDs from a team. Accepts either a raw numeric project ID or any Figma URL containing a '/project/<projectId>' path segment.

projectIdOrUrl.description: Numeric Figma project ID, or a Figma URL containing '/project/<projectId>'

tools.name: figma_get_file_comments
tools.description: Get all comments left on a Figma design file, including comment text, author, and creation date. Accepts either a raw Figma file key or a full Figma design file URL.

href.description: Figma design file URL, or a raw Figma file key

tools.name: testrail_test
tools.description: Test TestRail connectivity by fetching projects

tools.name: testrail_get_projects
tools.description: Get list of all projects in TestRail

tools.name: testrail_get_suites
tools.description: Get all test suites for a TestRail project

project_name.description: Project name to get suites from

tools.name: testrail_get_case
tools.description: Get a TestRail test case by ID. Set format='markdown' to receive preconditions/steps/expected-result HTML fields converted to clean Markdown (tables preserved as GitHub-Flavoured Markdown) instead of raw TestRail HTML — raw HTML pasted from Google Docs/browsers can be 20-30x larger than necessary due to inline CSS styling on every tag.

case_id.description: The test case ID (numeric, without 'C' prefix)

format.description: Output format for HTML-bearing fields (preconditions, steps, expected results): 'html' (default, raw TestRail HTML) or 'md'/'markdown' (cleaned Markdown — much smaller and easier to read or feed to an LLM). If omitted, falls back to the TESTRAIL_DEFAULT_FORMAT env var (defaults to 'html' when unset).

tools.name: testrail_get_all_cases
tools.description: Get ALL test cases in a project (uses pagination to retrieve all cases). Set format='markdown' to receive preconditions/steps/expected-result HTML fields converted to clean Markdown (tables preserved as GitHub-Flavoured Markdown) instead of raw TestRail HTML.

project_name.description: Project name to get all cases from

format.description: Output format for HTML-bearing fields (preconditions, steps, expected results): 'html' (default, raw TestRail HTML) or 'md'/'markdown' (cleaned Markdown — much smaller and easier to read or feed to an LLM). If omitted, falls back to the TESTRAIL_DEFAULT_FORMAT env var (defaults to 'html' when unset).

tools.name: testrail_search_cases
tools.description: Search TestRail test cases by project and optional filters. Set format='markdown' to receive preconditions/steps/expected-result HTML fields converted to clean Markdown (tables preserved as GitHub-Flavoured Markdown) instead of raw TestRail HTML.

format.description: Output format for HTML-bearing fields (preconditions, steps, expected results): 'html' (default, raw TestRail HTML) or 'md'/'markdown' (cleaned Markdown — much smaller and easier to read or feed to an LLM). If omitted, falls back to the TESTRAIL_DEFAULT_FORMAT env var (defaults to 'html' when unset).

tools.name: testrail_get_cases_by_refs
tools.description: Get test cases linked to a requirement/story via refs field

tools.name: testrail_create_case
tools.description: Create a new test case in TestRail

description.example:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Enter credentials
3. Click login

tools.name: testrail_create_case_detailed
tools.description: Create a new test case in TestRail with detailed fields (preconditions, steps, expected results, labels, type). Note: TestRail uses its own table format in text fields: |||:Col 1|:Col 2|:Col 3\n||val1|val2|val3. Standard Markdown tables (| Col | Col |) will be auto-converted to TestRail format.

preconditions.description: Preconditions (optional). For tables use TestRail format: |||:Col1|:Col2\n||val1|val2

type_id.description: Case type ID (optional). Use testrail_get_case_types to get available types.

label_ids.description: Comma-separated label IDs (optional). Use testrail_get_labels to find IDs.

expected.example: User is logged in and redirected to dashboard

steps.description: Test steps separated by double newline (optional)
steps.example:
Navigate to login page.

Enter username %Username%.

Click Login button.

tools.name: testrail_create_case_steps
tools.description: Create a TestRail test case using the 'Test Case (Steps)' template (template_id=2). Steps are provided as a JSON array: [{"content":"step text","expected":"expected result"}, ...]. Markdown tables in step content or expected are auto-converted to HTML tables. Use testrail_get_case_types for type_id, testrail_get_labels for label_ids.

type_id.description: Case type ID (optional). Use testrail_get_case_types to get available types.

label_ids.description: Comma-separated label IDs (optional). Use testrail_get_labels to find IDs.

steps_json.description: JSON array of step objects: [{"content":"step","expected":"result"}, ...]. Markdown tables are auto-converted to HTML.
steps_json.example: [{"content":"Open login page","expected":"Login form is displayed"},{"content":"Enter credentials","expected":"Fields populated"}]

tools.name: testrail_update_case
tools.description: Update a test case in TestRail

case_id.description: The test case ID to update

tools.name: testrail_delete_case
tools.description: Delete a test case in TestRail by case ID

case_id.description: The numeric test case ID to delete (without the C prefix)

tools.name: testrail_link_to_requirement
tools.description: Link a test case to a requirement by updating refs field

tools.name: testrail_get_labels
tools.description: Get all labels for a project in TestRail

tools.name: testrail_get_label
tools.description: Get a single label by ID

tools.name: testrail_update_label
tools.description: Update a label title in TestRail. Maximum 20 characters allowed.

tools.name: testrail_get_case_types
tools.description: Get all available case types in TestRail (e.g., Automated, Functionality, Other)

tools.name: kb_get
tools.description: Get last sync date for a knowledge base source. Returns ISO 8601 date string or 'Source not found' message.

output_path.description: Optional path to KB directory. Defaults to DMTOOLS_KB_OUTPUT_PATH env var or current directory

tools.name: kb_build
tools.description: Build or update knowledge base from input file. Processes chat messages, documentation, or any text data to create indexed, searchable knowledge base. Returns JSON with statistics.

input_file.description: Path to input file (JSON or text format). Can be JSON array of messages or plain text.

output_path.description: Optional path to KB directory. Defaults to DMTOOLS_KB_OUTPUT_PATH env var or current directory

clean_source.description: Optional. If true, removes all existing Q/A/N from this source before processing. Use for content refresh (e.g., Confluence pages).

tools.name: kb_process
tools.description: Process input file and build KB structure WITHOUT generating AI descriptions (fast mode). Use this for bulk data processing. Run kb_aggregate later to generate descriptions.

input_file.description: Path to input file (JSON or text format). Can be JSON array of messages or plain text.

output_path.description: Optional path to KB directory. Defaults to DMTOOLS_KB_OUTPUT_PATH env var or current directory

clean_source.description: Optional. If true, removes all existing Q/A/N from this source before processing. Use for content refresh (e.g., Confluence pages).

tools.name: kb_aggregate
tools.description: Generate AI descriptions for existing KB structure WITHOUT processing new data. Use this after kb_process to add AI-generated descriptions for people and topics.

smart_mode.description: Only regenerate descriptions if Q/A/N files have changed. Default: true

output_path.description: Optional path to KB directory. Defaults to DMTOOLS_KB_OUTPUT_PATH env var or current directory

source_name.description: Optional: Filter to only regenerate for specific source. If null/empty, regenerates for ALL sources (recommended for description regeneration).

tools.name: kb_process_inbox
tools.description: Scan inbox/raw/ folders and process all unprocessed files automatically. Files are processed in place. Returns JSON with processed and skipped file details.

generate_descriptions.description: Generate AI descriptions after processing. Default: true

output_path.description: Optional path to KB directory. Defaults to DMTOOLS_KB_OUTPUT_PATH env var or current directory

smart_aggregation.description: Only regenerate descriptions if Q/A/N changed. Default: true

tools.name: teams_auth_start
tools.description: Start device code authentication for Microsoft Teams. Returns URL and code for user to authenticate.

tools.name: teams_auth_complete
tools.description: Complete device code authentication after user approval. Polls for tokens and saves refresh token.

tools.name: teams_auth_status
tools.description: Check current Microsoft Teams authentication status and configuration.

tools.name: gitlab_test
tools.description: Test GitLab connectivity by fetching the current user's profile

tools.name: gitlab_get_mr_comments
tools.description: Get all comments for a GitLab merge request, including both inline code review comments (DiffNote) and general discussion notes. Excludes system-generated notes.

tools.name: gitlab_add_mr_label
tools.description: Add a label to a GitLab merge request.

tools.name: gitlab_remove_mr_label
tools.description: Remove a label from a GitLab merge request.

tools.name: gitlab_get_mr_activities
tools.description: Get all activities for a GitLab merge request. Approvals are fetched from the real GitLab Approvals API. General discussion notes are also included.

tools.name: gitlab_add_mr_comment
tools.description: Add a general discussion comment to a GitLab merge request.

tools.name: gitlab_get_mr_diff
tools.description: Get diff stats and changed files for a GitLab merge request.

tools.name: gitlab_get_mr_diff_text
tools.description: Get the raw unified diff text for a GitLab merge request (suitable for locating file/line positions, e.g. for inline review comments).

tools.name: gitlab_get_mr
tools.description: Get details of a specific GitLab merge request including title, description, state, author, diff_refs (base_sha, head_sha, start_sha needed for inline comments).

tools.name: gitlab_list_mrs
tools.description: List merge requests for a GitLab project. State can be 'opened', 'closed', 'merged', or 'all'.

state.description: MR state: opened, closed, merged, all. 'open' is also accepted as a synonym for 'opened'.

tools.name: gitlab_create_mr
tools.description: Create a GitLab merge request from a source branch into a target branch.

tools.name: gitlab_get_mr_discussions
tools.description: Get all discussion threads for a GitLab merge request. Each discussion contains notes (comments) and a resolved status. Use the discussion id with gitlab_resolve_mr_thread.

tools.name: gitlab_reply_to_mr_thread
tools.description: Reply to an existing discussion thread in a GitLab merge request. Use the discussion id from gitlab_get_mr_discussions.

tools.name: gitlab_add_inline_mr_comment
tools.description: Create a new inline code review comment on a specific file and line in a GitLab merge request. Requires base_sha, head_sha, start_sha from the MR diff refs (use gitlab_get_mr to get them from diff_refs).

line.description: Line number in the new file to comment on

startSha.description: Start commit SHA from MR diff_refs

filePath.description: Path to the file to comment on

baseSha.description: Base commit SHA from MR diff_refs

headSha.description: Head commit SHA from MR diff_refs

tools.name: gitlab_resolve_mr_thread
tools.description: Resolve (close) a review discussion thread in a GitLab merge request. Use the discussion id from gitlab_get_mr_discussions.

tools.name: gitlab_approve_mr
tools.description: Approve a GitLab merge request. Adds your approval to the MR.

tools.name: gitlab_merge_mr
tools.description: Merge a GitLab merge request. Optionally provide a custom merge commit message.

tools.name: gitlab_rebase_mr
tools.description: Ask GitLab to rebase/update a merge request source branch with its target branch.

tools.name: gitlab_list_project_jobs
tools.description: List recent GitLab CI jobs for a project.

tools.name: gitlab_cancel_job
tools.description: Cancel a GitLab CI job.

tools.name: gitlab_list_pipeline_runs
tools.description: List recent GitLab CI pipelines. Optionally filter by status, ref, and limit.

limit.description: Maximum number of pipelines to return

tools.name: gitlab_trigger_pipeline

tools.name: gitlab_get_commit_statuses
tools.description: Get CI/CD statuses for a commit SHA in a GitLab project. Returns one entry per status report (e.g. posted by an external CI like Jenkins via the gitlabBuilds plugin, or GitLab's own pipeline). Equivalent of GitHub's 'check runs' for GitLab. When the same status name was reported more than once for this commit (e.g. a CI job was retried), only the most recent report per name is returned.

commitSha.description: The commit SHA to get statuses for

tools.name: gitlab_get_job_logs
tools.description: Get GitLab CI job trace logs.

tools.name: gitlab_get_or_create_release
tools.description: Find an existing GitLab release by tag, or create one if it does not exist. Useful for a stable artefact storage release (mirrors github_get_or_create_draft_release). Note: GitLab releases have no draft concept; releases are visible as soon as they are created.

tagName.description: The Git tag name for the release. Reused to find an existing release.

targetCommitish.description: Optional branch or commit SHA the release's tag should point to when created (GitLab 'ref'). Required if the tag does not already exist.

body.example: Internal storage release for PR attachments.

releaseName.description: The human-readable release name. If empty, tagName is used.

tools.name: gitlab_upload_release_asset
tools.description: Upload a local file as a GitLab release asset. Internally publishes the file to the project's Generic Package Registry and attaches it to the release as an asset link, so it is discoverable the same way as a GitHub release asset. Returns the release link metadata including direct_asset_url. Set overwrite=true to automatically replace an existing asset with the same name before uploading (GitLab's Generic Package Registry rejects duplicate uploads with 409 Conflict otherwise).

filePath.description: Absolute or relative path to the local file to upload.

assetName.description: Optional asset filename shown in GitLab. Defaults to the local filename.

packageName.description: Optional Generic Package Registry package name used to group the uploaded files. Defaults to 'release-assets'.

tagName.description: The tag name of the release returned by gitlab_get_or_create_release.

contentType.description: Optional MIME type. Defaults to detected type or application/octet-stream.

overwrite.description: If true, delete any existing asset (release link + underlying generic package file) with the same name before uploading. Defaults to false.

tools.name: gitlab_list_release_assets
tools.description: List all assets (asset links) attached to a GitLab release. Returns a JSON array of link objects including id, name, url, and direct_asset_url.

tagName.description: The tag name of the release.

tools.name: gitlab_delete_release_asset
tools.description: Delete a GitLab release asset by its asset name. Removes both the release link and the underlying Generic Package Registry file. Use gitlab_list_release_assets to find asset names.

assetName.description: The name of the asset to delete, as returned by gitlab_list_release_assets.

packageName.description: Optional Generic Package Registry package name the asset was uploaded under. Defaults to 'release-assets'.

tagName.description: The tag name of the release.

tools.name: gitlab_download_release_asset
tools.description: Download a GitLab release asset (a file published to the Generic Package Registry and attached to a release) to a local file path.

assetName.description: The name of the asset to download, as returned by gitlab_list_release_assets.

targetFilePath.description: Local file path where the downloaded asset should be saved.

packageName.description: Optional Generic Package Registry package name the asset was uploaded under. Defaults to 'release-assets'.

tagName.description: The tag name of the release.

newProjectKey.description: Key for the new project, e.g. TP2

tools.name: jira_setup_project_workflow
tools.description: Set up a project's workflow using an explicit list of statuses (no source project needed). Accepts a JSON array of {name, category} objects and applies them to the target project's workflow. Valid categories: TODO, IN_PROGRESS, DONE. Works with team-managed (next-gen) Jira projects.

projectKey.description: Key of the target Jira project, e.g. TP

statusDefinitions.description: JSON array of status definitions, e.g. [{"name":"Backlog","category":"TODO"},...]. Valid categories: TODO, IN_PROGRESS, DONE.

tools.name: jira_sync_project_workflow
tools.description: Sync the workflow (statuses) of a target project to exactly match the source project using Jira's bulk workflow update API. Replaces the target workflow's statuses and transitions with those from the source project. Existing issues with removed statuses are automatically migrated to the closest matching new status. Works with team-managed (next-gen) Jira projects.

sourceProjectKey.description: Key of the source project to copy workflow from, e.g. MYTUBE

targetProjectKey.description: Key of the target project to update workflow for, e.g. TP
